title: WalletMasterKey::as_bytes() returns plain [u8;64] copy of spending key
description: core/src/wallet.rs:69 WalletMasterKey::as_bytes() copies spending_key and chain_code into a plain [u8;64] that is not Zeroizing. Called by tests and by the CLI key-separation check (commands.rs:3977). The returned array is not zeroized when the caller's binding drops. Fix: change return type to Zeroizing<[u8;64]>. spending_key_bytes() returns &[u8;32] (reference, not copy) so it is safe; only as_bytes() creates an unzeroized copy.
